summary: An excellent matrix capture of the July‑16 1976 show delivers crystal‑clear sound and tight playing, highlighted by Cosmic Charlie, High Time and an inspiring rendition of The Wheel.
review: The matrix source is praised for flawless tape sync, rich instrument separation and a vivid audience feel, making the recording sound almost live.  Musically the concert shines with a jazzy, unpredictable set‑list – the second half especially smokes on Cosmic Charlie, High Time and a lyrical The Wheel, while the band stays in a tight groove throughout.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4–5 stars, praising both the performance and the matrix recording; weighted scoring of show (≈4.7) and recording (≈4.8) yields an overall rating around 4.7.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Perfectly aligned tapes with no delay or fade, rich full‑band sound, clear instrument separation; occasional comment about odd vocal looping on the Stronger Than Dirt jam but otherwise pristine.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Unpredictable, jazzy set with many “nuggets”; a strong, smoking second half that stays in a tight groove and delivers memorable transitions.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar work is described as kind‑vibe, lyrical (especially on The Wheel) and consistently solid throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass lines at the start of the jam receive special notice – punchy and suggestive of the Stronger Than Dirt theme.
band_member_comments.Keys: Donna Jean’s vocals are highlighted as beautiful in the mix; Keith’s keyboards add occasional Slipknot‑like hints during jams.
band_member_comments.Drums: The two drummers are praised for being tightly tuned to each other and maintaining a flawless groove.
